title: Friction and Connected Particles
description: Model friction as a resistive force with the F ≤ μN law, apply F = ma to particles decelerating on rough horizontal and inclined surfaces, and analyse systems of connected particles linked by a light inextensible string over a pulley.
objectives: Explain friction as a resistive force opposing relative motion or the tendency to move
Apply the limiting friction relation F ≤ μN, with F = μN at the point of slipping
Apply F = ma to particles decelerating due to friction on horizontal and inclined surfaces
Set up F = ma for each particle in a system connected by a light inextensible string over a pulley
Solve simultaneous equations of motion using a common tension T and common acceleration magnitude a

lessons.title: Friction and the F ≤ μN model
F = ma with friction on horizontal and inclined surfaces
Connected particles: strings, pulleys and simultaneous equations
